label: Micro-randomized Trials with Categorical Treatments: Causal Effect Estimation an
authors: Jeremy Lin, Tianchen Qian
headline: Extended WCLS and sample size formulas to multi-categorical MRT treatments
detail: Extends causal excursion effect definition and WCLS estimator to MRTs with categorical (3+) treatment levels. Develops sample size formula for comparing categorical treatment levels with type I error and power guarantees. Provides practical guidelines. Illustrated on HeartSteps. Directly relevant: 4-action MDP requires multi-categorical treatment methods. (Found in missions: 02)

label: Semiparametric Off-Policy Inference for Optimal Policy Values under Possible Non
authors: Haoyu Wei
headline: Developed NSAVE method for off-policy inference with Drink Less MRT application
detail: Studies inference for optimal policy values in MDPs. Proposes Nonparametric Sequential Value Evaluation (NSAVE) method yielding martingale-based inference with double-robustness. Applies to Drink Less MRT for confidence intervals on state-adaptive notification policies. Relevant for off-policy evaluation when transitioning from MRT to RL-based policies. (Found in missions: 02)

label: Dynamic Causal Mediation Analysis for Intensive Longitudinal Data
authors: Tianchen Qian
headline: Introduced natural direct/indirect excursion effects for mediation in MRTs
detail: Proposes natural direct and indirect excursion effects to decompose total excursion effects in MRTs. Derives efficient influence functions and multiply-robust estimators. Applied to HeartSteps MRT and SleepHealth observational study. Bridges MRT causal inference with mediation analysis for understanding why interventions work. (Found in missions: 02)

label: Robust Estimation of Moderated Causal Excursion Odds Ratio in Micro-Randomized T
authors: Jiaxin Yu, Tianchen Qian
headline: Doubly robust estimators for moderated causal excursion odds ratio in MRTs
detail: Two estimators for moderated causal excursion odds ratio for binary outcomes. First is doubly robust when moderator captures sequential randomization; second handles general settings with robustness to association model misspecification. Applied to Drink Less MRT. Provides odds-ratio scale alternative to relative risk for binary proximal outcomes. (Found in missions: 02)

label: Doubly Robust Estimation with Stabilized Weights for Binary Proximal Outcomes in
authors: Jinho Cha, Eunchan Cha
headline: DR-EMEE achieves 2x efficiency gain over IPW and 5-10% over EMEE for binary MRT outcomes
detail: Proposes doubly robust EMEE with stabilized and truncated weights for binary proximal outcomes. Proves double robustness, asymptotic efficiency. Up to twofold efficiency gains over IPW. Validated on HeartSteps, PAMAP2, and mHealth datasets. Practical efficiency gains for binary outcomes common in mHealth engagement metrics. (Found in missions: 02)

label: Power Constrained Nonstationary Bandits with Habituation and Recovery Dynamics
authors: Fengxu Li, Stephanie M. Carpenter, Matthew P. Buman, Yonatan Mintz
headline: Thompson Sampling with probability clipping balances personalization and power in MRTs
detail: Develops ROGUE-TS for nonstationary bandits with habituation/recovery dynamics. Introduces probability clipping to balance personalization and population-level learning. Validated on two MRT datasets (physical activity, bipolar disorder). Maintains statistical power while lowering regret. Directly relevant to power-constrained exploration. Bridges bandit algorithms and MRT statistical validity. (Found in missions: 02)

label: Prediction Intervals for Individual Treatment Effects in a Multiple Decision Poi
authors: Swaraj Bose, Walter Dempsey
headline: Conformal prediction intervals for time-varying ITEs in MRTs with non-exchangeability guarantees
detail: Proposes conformal inference method for prediction intervals of time-varying individual treatment effects. Weaker exchangeability assumptions. Coverage guarantee dependent on non-exchangeability degree. Applied to Intern Health Study MRT. Extends MRT inference from average to individual-level effects with uncertainty quantification. (Found in missions: 02)

label: Evaluating time-varying treatment effects in hybrid SMART-MRT designs
authors: Mengbing Li, Inbal Nahum-Shani, Walter Dempsey
headline: First causal estimands and analysis methods for hybrid SMART+MRT designs
detail: Formally defines synergistic effects in hybrid SMART-MRT designs integrating human-delivered and digital components at different timescales. Assesses proximal and distal outcomes. Applied to M-Bridge targeting binge drinking reduction in college students. Relevant for multi-timescale adaptation combining coach and digital components. (Found in missions: 02)

label: Estimation of Time-Varying Treatment Effects in a Joint Model for Longitudinal a
authors: Madeline R Abbott, Jeremy M G Taylor, Inbal Nahum-Shani, Lindsey N Potter, David W Wetter, Cho Y Lam, Walter Dempsey
headline: Bayesian joint model handles MRT treatment effects on longitudinal and recurrent event outcomes
detail: Model-based approach for MRT treatment effect estimation via joint longitudinal-survival model. Bayesian inference with information criteria. Demonstrated on MRT of substance use with multiple outcomes and recurrent events. Joint modeling approach for complex MRT outcomes beyond simple proximal responses. (Found in missions: 02)

label: IntelligentPooling: Practical Thompson Sampling for mHealth
authors: Sabina Tomkins, Peng Liao, Predrag Klasnja, Susan Murphy
headline: Practical Thompson sampling contextual bandit for mHealth, deployed in a clinical trial.
detail: Proposes IntelligentPooling for small sample sizes, non-stationarity, and delayed rewards in mHealth. Core methodology from Murphy lab. Foundation for mHealth bandit deployments. (Found in missions: 03)

label: A Contextual-Bandit-Based Approach for Informed Decision-Making in Clinical Tria
authors: Yogatheesan Varatharajah, Brent Berry
headline: CB with Thompson sampling outperforms random assignment in stroke clinical trial simulation.
detail: Evaluated on International Stroke Trial data. 72.63% gain vs random, 64.34% gain vs context-free MAB. Demonstrates clinical trial optimization using patient contexts. (Found in missions: 03, 04)

label: Brief Digital Interventions for Psychological Distress: An AI-Enhanced Response-
authors: Jill Newby, Sunil Gupta, Leonard Hoon, et al.
headline: Contextual MAB allocated 1,282 students to digital mental health interventions; physical activity best for severe distress.
detail: AI-enhanced response-adaptive RCT. Physical activity and mindfulness effective for severe distress; physical activity and sleep hygiene for mild distress. Improved trial efficiency. Landmark bandit clinical trial in mental health. Top medical journal. (Found in missions: 03)

label: Protocol for the Vibe Up bandit-based adaptive trial for psychological distress
authors: Kit Huckvale, Leonard Hoon, Eileen Stech, et al.
headline: Protocol for contextual bandit AI-adaptive trial comparing digital interventions for student distress.
detail: Multiarm contextual bandit with up to 12 sequential mini-trials comparing mindfulness, physical activity, sleep hygiene, and control. Protocol for Vibe Up. Companion to the JAMA Network Open results. (Found in missions: 03)

label: CAREForMe: Contextual MAB Recommendation Framework for Mental Health
authors: Sheng Yu, Narjes Nourzad, Randye J. Semple, et al.
headline: Contextual MAB framework for mental health with mobile sensing and online learning.
detail: Integrates mobile sensing, online learning, and user clustering. Implemented across Discord and Telegram. Framework paper; no clinical deployment. (Found in missions: 03)

label: Using Adaptive Bandit Experiments to Increase Engagement in Mental Health
authors: Harsh Kumar, Tong Li, Jiakai Shi, et al.
headline: Thompson Sampling bandit deployed in text-message mental health intervention with 1,100 users.
detail: Two-year system development. Recruited via large mental health non-profit. Compares bandit with uniform random. Large-scale mental health deployment. Mohr and Williams labs. (Found in missions: 03)

label: Offline Contextual MAB for mHealth: A Case Study on Emotion Regulation
authors: Mawulolo K. Ameko, Miranda L. Beltzer, Lihua Cai, et al.
headline: First offline CB recommender for emotion regulation using real mobile health data (n=114).
detail: Doubly robust offline learning algorithms significantly outperformed baselines for emotion regulation strategy recommendation. Early CB work for mental health. Transdiagnostic emotion regulation focus. (Found in missions: 03)

label: Exploring RL for Fluid Transitions Between Clinical Mental Healthcare and Everyd
authors: Tony Wang, Qian Yang
headline: [Mixed] Contextual bandit for clinical-wellness mental health transitions (N=38); benefits appeared only post-intervention.
detail: Contextual bandit selecting journaling prompts from clinical and wellness repertoires. Benefits of RL optimization emerged only after interventions ended. Raises questions about burnout and treatment intensity. Important negative finding: bandit-optimized sequences showed delayed benefits. (Found in missions: 03)

label: Personalized Glucose Management With AI: Pilot Study Using a Multiarmed Bandit A
authors: Shinji Hotta, Mikko Kytö, Saila Koivusalo, Seppo Heinonen, Pekka Marttinen
headline: Multi-armed bandit for postprandial glucose management shows 23% improvement in real-world pilot (n=6).
detail: Two-stage reward prediction model predicts dietary and exercise effects on glucose. Simulation study plus 6-person pilot. Early-stage diabetes bandit. Small but promising pilot. (Found in missions: 03)

label: Equitable Intervention Planning with RMAB for Type 2 Diabetes Digital Health
authors: Jackson A. Killian, Manish Jain, Yugang Jia, et al.
headline: Restless MAB with equity constraints for T2D: 10% more patients reach targets, 85% less disparity.
detail: RMAB with equitable bilevel optimization. 10% improvement in glucose targets, 10% reduction in dropout, 85% reduction in disparities across 6 demographic groups. Important equity-focused bandit. Google-Verily-Harvard collaboration. (Found in missions: 03)

label: Practical considerations for online learning algorithm in app-based mHealth (LOW
authors: Rachel T. Gonzalez, Madeline R. Abbott, Brahmajee Nallamothu, et al.
headline: LOWSALT4LIFE 2 trial: contextual bandit for hypertension app engagement; deployment challenges documented.
detail: RL algorithm sends notifications to reduce sodium intake. Documents challenges: reward definition, timescale, model specification, missing data, computational cost. Important practical deployment paper. Hypertension domain. University of Michigan. (Found in missions: 03, 04)

label: REINFORCE trial: RL to personalize communication on medication adherence
authors: John C. Lauffenburger, Elad Yom-Tov, Punam A. Keller, et al.
headline: REINFORCE trial: RL-personalized communication significantly improved medication adherence at 6 months.
detail: Multi-arm bandit RL trial testing personalized messages for medication adherence. Found significant improvements over 6-month follow-up. Landmark bandit trial for medication adherence. Top digital health journal. (Found in missions: 03)

label: RL for persuading to quit smoking and be physically active
authors: N. Albers, M.A. Neerincx, W.P. Brinkman
headline: RL algorithm modeling current and future states for smoking cessation and physical activity persuasion.
detail: Models both current and future user states to personalize smoking cessation and physical activity persuasion strategies. Smoking cessation bandit/RL. Combines smoking and physical activity. (Found in missions: 03)

label: A Bayesian-bandit adaptive design for N-of-1 clinical trials
authors: S. Shrestha, S. Jain
headline: Thompson sampling bandit adaptive design for N-of-1 clinical trials.
detail: Bayesian bandit using TS for individualized treatment comparisons within single patients. N-of-1 trial design. Relevant for personalized medicine. (Found in missions: 03, 04)

label: Multi-disciplinary fairness considerations in ML for clinical trials
authors: Irene Chien, Nina Deliu, Rebecca Turner, Adrian Weller, Sofia Villar, et al.
headline: Multidisciplinary analysis of fairness in ML for clinical trials, including bandit algorithms.
detail: Addresses fairness in bandit algorithm design, multiple testing, and ethical guidelines for clinical trials. Directly addresses ethical concerns with bandits in clinical trials. (Found in missions: 03)

label: Truthful mechanisms for linear bandit games with private contexts
authors: Yiting Hu, Lingjie Duan
headline: Shows UCB and TS fail under context misreporting in clinical trials; proposes truthful mechanism.
detail: First study of private context misreporting in CB. Shows standard algorithms fail in clinical trial settings. Proposes LP-based mechanism with O(ln T) regret. Important negative result: failure modes of standard bandits under strategic misreporting. (Found in missions: 03)

label: A New Bandit Setting Balancing State Evolution and Corrupted Context
authors: Alexander Galozy, Slawomir Nowaczyk, Mattias Ohlsson
headline: Novel bandit combining contextual and state-transition info, evaluated on simulated medication adherence.
detail: Combines contextual bandit and MAB via referee. Motivated by mHealth where user internal states are partially observable. Handles partially observable internal states and unreliable context in mHealth. (Found in missions: 03)

label: Optimal dose selection in phase I/II trials with contextual bandits
authors: Jixian Wang, Ram Tiwari
headline: CB with TS for oncology dose-finding; practical recommendations for industry adoption.
detail: Extensive simulation comparing TS, Bayesian bootstrap, and MAB approaches for dose-finding. Practical recommendations provided. BMS paper showing industry CB adoption for dose-finding. (Found in missions: 03, 04)

label: Learn to Intervene: Adaptive Learning for Restless Bandits in Preventive Healthc
authors: Arpita Biswas, Gaurav Aggarwal, Pradeep Varakantham, Milind Tambe
headline: Restless bandit adaptive policy for medication adherence in diabetes and hypertension.
detail: Adaptive learning policy for health intervention allocation in resource-constrained settings. Evaluated on Chiapas Mexico data. Tambe lab. Restless bandits for global health. (Found in missions: 03)

label: Optimizing HIV Patient Engagement with RL in Resource-Limited Settings
authors: Álvaro Periáñez, K. Schmitz, L. Makhupula, et al.
headline: Bandit algorithms for HIV patient engagement and treatment adherence in underserved populations.
detail: Applies bandit algorithms to optimize HIV patient engagement in resource-limited Sub-Saharan African contexts. Global health bandit application for HIV. (Found in missions: 03)

label: Spoiled for choice? Personalized recommendation for healthcare: A multiarmed ban
authors: Tao Zhou, Yuqing Wang, Lu Yan, Yong Tan
headline: Contextual bandit with diversity constraints for healthcare decision recommendations.
detail: Multiarmed bandit for personalized healthcare recommendations with diversity constraints. Information systems perspective. IS perspective on CB for healthcare recommendations. (Found in missions: 03)

label: Risk-inclusive Contextual Bandits for Early Phase Clinical Trials
authors: R. Kanrar, C. Li, Z. Ghodsi, M. Gamalo
headline: Risk-inclusive CB with Thompson sampling for early phase clinical trials (Pfizer).
detail: Multi-objective optimization with risk constraints. Leverages TS and online RL for early phase trials. Industry (Pfizer) risk-aware bandits for clinical trials. (Found in missions: 03)

label: Optimizing Digital Therapeutic Interventions under Endogenous Adherence
authors: E. Pulick, S. Carpenter, M. Buman, Y. Mintz
headline: UCB-BOLD: contextual bandit for digital therapeutics accounting for endogenous adherence.
detail: Addresses the challenge that adherence is affected by the treatment itself (endogeneity). Addresses endogeneity challenge in digital therapeutic bandits. (Found in missions: 03)

label: Planning to fairly allocate: Probabilistic fairness in restless bandits
authors: Christina Herlihy, A. Prins, A. Srinivasan, et al.
headline: Probabilistic fairness framework for restless bandits in health intervention allocation.
detail: Addresses ethical concerns about disparities from bandit-based health resource allocation. Provides fairness guarantees. Fairness/ethics of bandit allocation in healthcare. (Found in missions: 03)

label: Accelerated TS for Response-Adaptive Trials
authors: Wang J
headline: [Mixed] NEGATIVE: TS disappointing in clinical trials; acceleration proposed
detail: Reports multiple simulations show TS fails in clinical trial settings. Proposes accelerated TS for short-run improvement. (Found in missions: 04)

label: Top-Two TS for Best Treatment ID
authors: Wang J, Tiwari R
headline: TTTS with acceleration for small-sample drug development
detail: Extends TTTS to drug development small-sample regime. Introduces simpler TTTS2 variant. (Found in missions: 04)

label: TS for SMART Designs
authors: Norwood P, Davidian M, Laber E
headline: First TS-based RAR for multi-stage SMARTs
detail: First RAR algorithms for SMARTs using TS. Valid post-study inference for non-standard limiting behavior. (Found in missions: 04)

label: Finite-Sample Error for RAR
authors: Deliu N, Villar SS
headline: [Mixed] When TS-based inference fails: finite-sample analysis
detail: Error control of randomization tests under TS-based RAR. Conditions where standard inference fails identified. (Found in missions: 04)

label: MABs for Dose-Finding
authors: Kojima M
headline: TS vs UCB vs Gittins for dose-finding trials
detail: Systematic MAB evaluation including TS for dose-finding. TS balances patient benefit and correct ID. (Found in missions: 04)

label: Replicable Bandits for Digital Health
authors: Zhang KW, Closser A, Trella AL, Murphy SA
headline: TS variants ensuring valid post-study inference
detail: Replicable bandits including TS variants guaranteeing error control for treatment effect estimation. (Found in missions: 04)

label: Causal Inference and Bandits for Digital Health
authors: Svihrova R, Dei Rossi A, Marzorati D, et al.
headline: [Mixed] Review: TS open problems in digital health
detail: Reviews TS variants, exploration, causal inference. Open problems: non-stationarity, heterogeneity. (Found in missions: 04)

label: TS for TB Screening in Peru
authors: Zhang J, Linde L, Puma D, et al.
headline: Deployed TS for mobile TB screening in Lima
detail: TS-based bandit optimizes mobile TB screening locations. Increases case detection efficiency. (Found in missions: 04)

label: Bayesian RAR for Cluster RCTs
authors: Liu Y, Young Karris M, Jain S
headline: TS-based RAR extended to cluster-randomized trials
detail: Extends Bayesian RAR with TS to CRCTs addressing intra-cluster correlation. (Found in missions: 04)

label: TS for Non-Stationary Bandits
authors: Qi H, Guo F, Zhu L
headline: TS with change-point detection for non-stationarity
detail: TS with change-point detection and sliding-window posteriors. Provides regret bounds. (Found in missions: 04)

label: Bandits for COVID-19 Case-Finding
authors: Rayo MF, Faulkner D, Kline D, et al.
headline: TS deployed for COVID-19 testing in Ohio
detail: TS bandits for adaptive testing site allocation. Improves over heuristic methods. (Found in missions: 04)

label: Bandits with Volume Constraints
authors: Warren JL, Prunas O, Paltiel AD, et al.
venue: Journal of the Royal Statistical Society: Series A
headline: TS with resource constraints for surveillance
detail: Integrates testing volume constraints into TS bandits for disease surveillance. (Found in missions: 04)

label: Bayesian Optimization for TMS
authors: Schultheiss DL, Turi Z, Boedecker J, Vlachos A
headline: Bayesian optimization reduces TMS patient burden
detail: GP-based Bayesian optimization for TMS coil positioning; fewer stimulations than grid search. (Found in missions: 04)

label: Bayesian m-Top for Vaccine Policy
authors: Cimpean A, Verstraeten T, Willem L, et al.
headline: Bayesian exploration for COVID-19 vaccine policy
detail: Bayesian m-top exploration (TS-related) for vaccine allocation policy evaluation. (Found in missions: 04)

label: Optimal Allocation in Adaptive Designs
authors: Yi Y, Wang X
headline: Theoretical: TS vs optimal allocation targets
detail: Compares TS-based allocation to optimal Neyman allocation. (Found in missions: 04)

label: Bayesian Strategy for GP Prescribing
authors: Allender S, Hayward J, Gupta S, et al.
headline: Bayesian optimization for healthcare strategy
detail: GP-based Bayesian optimization identifies optimal healthcare strategies. (Found in missions: 04)

label: MAB Backfill BOIN
authors: Kojima M, Takeda K
headline: TS-MAB with Bayesian optimal interval design
detail: Integrates TS-based MAB with BOIN for dose-finding with backfill. (Found in missions: 04)

label: Mixed-Effects Bandit for mHealth
authors: Huch EK, Shi J, Abbott MR, et al.
headline: Mixed-effects TS for user heterogeneity in mHealth
detail: Robust mixed-effects TS accounting for between-user heterogeneity in mobile health. (Found in missions: 04)

label: Bayesian RAR with Binary Outcomes
authors: Proper J, Connett J, Murray T
headline: [Mixed] Comparison of Bayesian RAR including TS-like allocation
detail: Evaluates Bayesian RAR techniques for binary outcomes, comparing TS-like posterior probability allocation against alternatives. (Found in missions: 04)
